cases.title: Best-first backlog of small parallelizable refactors
cases.user_request: Find a handful of small, low-risk refactor tickets we could hand to agents in parallel.

notes: The user wants ticket-shaped recommendations, not a broad cleanup strategy.

static_checks: Each ticket reads like an executable task rather than a theme.
review_questions: Are the suggested refactors narrow enough to assign independently?
Does each ticket identify a concrete surface and expected validation?
Does the list avoid turning into architecture or style cleanup work?

cases.title: Tailor suggestions to a contributor when history is present
cases.user_request: Give me a shortlist of refactors that make sense for the area I've been touching recently.

notes: The response should explicitly connect fit-to-contributor when possible.

static_checks: Mentions whether contribution history was used or unavailable.
review_questions: Does the response tie at least some recommendations to the contributor's recent surfaces when that evidence exists?
Does it stay honest if the contribution signal is weak?
Are the tickets still low-risk and bounded even when tailored?

cases.title: Few good refactors should yield a short or empty list
cases.user_request: Find refactor opportunities, but only suggest items that are genuinely small and safe.

notes: The inspected surface may not contain many good candidates.
The correct answer might be a very short list.

static_checks: Avoids inventing weak refactors just to fill the list.
review_questions: Does the response stay selective instead of padding the list with mediocre ideas?
Does it explain why larger or riskier candidates were excluded?
Would the final shortlist be safe to hand off without re-scoping?
